title: prunus × lannesiana f. ohsibayama
intro: Prunus × lannesiana f. ohsibayama, also known as Japanese Apricot, is a cultivar of the apricot tree that is native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Prunus × lannesiana f. ohsibayama include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas and infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues.
